title: angylocalyx oligophyllus
intro: Angylocalyx oligophyllus, also known as the African cork tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic effects. The health benefits of Angylocalyx oligophyllus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to aid in the management of diabetes and hypertension. Herbal preparations of Angylocalyx oligophyllus, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, can be made from the bark, leaves, and roots of the plant, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
